title: Constraint domain classification + SolverRegistry dispatch
analysis:
The task requires three interlocking pieces: (1) a ConstraintClassifier that walks CompiledExpr trees and determines ConstraintDomain, (2) a connected-component decomposer that builds a bipartite graph of constraints ↔ auto params and finds independent sub-problems, and (3) a SolverRegistry that combines classification + decomposition to dispatch sub-problems to domain-specific solvers.

The existing codebase is well-structured for this. ConstraintDomain already exists in reify-types::constraint (Dimensional, Geometric, Logical, CrossDomain). The DimensionalSolver implements the ConstraintSolver trait. The Engine in reify-eval holds an Option<Box<dyn ConstraintSolver>> and calls .solve() on it. The SolverRegistry itself will implement ConstraintSolver, making it a drop-in replacement — the Engine just receives a SolverRegistry as its solver instead of a DimensionalSolver directly.

Classification logic: Walk the expression tree. Leaf types (Scalar with LENGTH/ANGLE dimensions → Dimensional, Bool/Int → Logical). If we encounter geometry function calls or geometry handle refs → Geometric. If mixed → CrossDomain. Since we don't yet have geometric constraints in the expression system (M5/M6), the classifier will classify most real constraints as Dimensional today, but the infrastructure must be ready.

Connected-component decomposition: Build a bipartite graph where constraints and auto params are nodes. An edge exists between a constraint and an auto param if the constraint's expression references that param (via collect_value_refs from reify-eval/src/deps.rs). Then use union-find to identify connected components. Each component is an independent ResolutionProblem that can be solved separately.

SolverRegistry implements ConstraintSolver by: (1) classifying each constraint, (2) decomposing into connected components, (3) determining domain for each component (unanimous domain → that domain; mixed → CrossDomain), (4) dispatching to appropriate solver, (5) merging results.

The compiler change is minimal: optionally tag CompiledConstraint with a ConstraintDomain field (computed lazily or at compile time). This is a non-breaking addition since the field can be Option<ConstraintDomain>.

Backward compatibility is maintained because SolverRegistry implements ConstraintSolver and the DimensionalSolver is used as both the dimensional and fallback solver. When no geometric/logical solvers are registered, everything falls back to the existing behavior.

prerequisites.description: Add new module files (classifier.rs, registry.rs, decompose.rs) to reify-constraints/src/lib.rs with pub mod declarations and re-exports
Add optional domain field to CompiledConstraint in reify-compiler: pub domain: Option<ConstraintDomain> (default None, non-breaking)

steps.description: Write failing tests for ConstraintClassifier: classify `thickness > 2mm` (Scalar comparisons) → Dimensional, classify `x == true` (Bool literal eq) → Logical, classify `x > 2mm && y == true` (mixed) → CrossDomain, classify pure arithmetic on dimensionless Reals → Dimensional, classify expression with geometry function call → Geometric. Tests in crates/reify-constraints/tests/classifier_tests.rs

Implement ConstraintClassifier in crates/reify-constraints/src/classifier.rs. Walk CompiledExpr tree, track leaf value types and operator types. Classification rules: (1) collect all leaf types via recursive traversal, (2) if any FunctionCall with geometry-related qualified_name → Geometric flag, (3) if all numeric Scalar leaves → Dimensional, (4) if all Bool/Int leaves with only logical ops → Logical, (5) if mixed flags → CrossDomain. Expose pub fn classify(expr: &CompiledExpr) -> ConstraintDomain

Write failing tests for connected-component decomposition: (1) 3 constraints each referencing a unique auto param → 3 components, (2) 2 constraints sharing one auto param → 1 component, (3) chain: C1 refs {a,b}, C2 refs {b,c} → 1 component with all 3 params, (4) empty constraints → 0 components, (5) constraint referencing no auto params → excluded from components. Tests in crates/reify-constraints/tests/decompose_tests.rs

Implement connected-component decomposition in crates/reify-constraints/src/decompose.rs. Define SubProblem struct {auto_params, constraints, domain: ConstraintDomain}. Use union-find: assign each auto param an index. For each constraint, collect ValueCellIds via expression tree walk (reuse collect_value_refs pattern), filter to auto param set, union all referenced params. Group constraints by component root. Determine per-component domain from individual constraint classifications (unanimous → that domain, mixed → CrossDomain). Return Vec<SubProblem>.

Write failing tests for SolverRegistry: (1) construct with DimensionalSolver as dimensional + fallback, no geometric/logical → dispatches to dimensional solver and gets same results as DimensionalSolver alone for a simple feasibility problem, (2) problem with no geometric solver registered → fallback used for geometric-classified constraints, (3) problem decomposed into 2 independent sub-problems → both solved, merged result contains all param values, (4) backward compat: existing auto resolution test cases (from solver_integration.rs) pass through SolverRegistry. Tests in crates/reify-constraints/tests/registry_tests.rs

Implement SolverRegistry in crates/reify-constraints/src/registry.rs. Struct holds dimensional: Box<dyn ConstraintSolver>, geometric: Option<Box<dyn ConstraintSolver>>, logical: Option<Box<dyn ConstraintSolver>>, fallback: Box<dyn ConstraintSolver>. Implement ConstraintSolver trait: (1) classify each constraint, (2) decompose into sub-problems, (3) for each sub-problem select solver by domain (geometric → geometric.unwrap_or(fallback), etc.), (4) construct ResolutionProblem per sub-problem with filtered auto_params and constraints and subset of current_values, (5) solve each, (6) merge SolveResult values into single HashMap, (7) if any sub-problem is Infeasible → whole is Infeasible, (8) handle objective by keeping it on the component that references the objective's expression vars.

Write test for cross-domain iteration: create a problem with 2 constraints — one Dimensional referencing params {a, b}, one that would be classified differently but shares param b. Verify SolverRegistry merges them into a single component (since they share b) and solves correctly via fallback.

Handle cross-domain components in SolverRegistry: when a component has CrossDomain classification, use the fallback (dimensional) solver. This is the correct behavior for now since dimensional solver handles numeric optimization generically. Add any edge-case handling discovered from step-7 test.

Write test for optional domain tagging in CompiledConstraint: verify that CompiledConstraint can be constructed with domain: None (backward compat) and domain: Some(ConstraintDomain::Dimensional). Verify existing compiler tests still pass with the new optional field.

Add domain: Option<ConstraintDomain> field to CompiledConstraint in reify-compiler/src/lib.rs. Initialize to None in compile_structure(). Update all existing construction sites. This is a pure addition — no existing behavior changes.

Write backward-compatibility integration test: run the full set of existing auto-resolution test scenarios (from solver_integration.rs) through a SolverRegistry wrapping DimensionalSolver. Verify identical results — Solved with same values, Infeasible for same infeasible problems.

Export all new public types from reify-constraints/src/lib.rs: ConstraintClassifier, SolverRegistry, SubProblem, decompose_into_components. Update lib.rs module declarations. Ensure cargo test passes for entire workspace.

design_decisions.rationale: This makes SolverRegistry a drop-in replacement for DimensionalSolver in the Engine. The Engine holds Option<Box<dyn ConstraintSolver>> and calls .solve() — no changes needed in reify-eval. Users can pass either DimensionalSolver or SolverRegistry.

design_decisions.decision: Union-find for connected components instead of BFS/DFS on adjacency list
design_decisions.rationale: Union-find is O(α(n)) per operation, simpler to implement correctly for bipartite constraint↔param graphs, and avoids building explicit adjacency lists. Well-suited for the merge-by-shared-param pattern.

design_decisions.decision: Classification walks the expression tree rather than relying on compiler-assigned domain tags
design_decisions.rationale: The classifier needs to work on arbitrary CompiledExpr at solve time, not just at compile time. The optional domain field on CompiledConstraint is for caching/hints, but the authoritative classification happens in the solver pipeline. This decouples the classifier from the compiler.

design_decisions.decision: CrossDomain components use the fallback (dimensional) solver
design_decisions.rationale: The dimensional solver (Nelder-Mead penalty method) is the most general solver — it handles arbitrary numeric constraints. When constraints span domains, falling back to it is safe. Future work can add cross-domain iteration strategies.

design_decisions.decision: Objective stays with the component containing its referenced variables
design_decisions.rationale: An optimization objective (Minimize/Maximize) only makes sense in the context of the sub-problem whose params it references. If the objective references params in multiple components, those components must be merged (they'll share params via the objective expression).

design_decisions.decision: New files (classifier.rs, registry.rs, decompose.rs) rather than expanding solver.rs
design_decisions.rationale: solver.rs is already 1600+ lines with the DimensionalSolver. Separate files maintain single-responsibility: classifier classifies, decomposer decomposes, registry orchestrates. Each is independently testable.

design_decisions.decision: Collect ValueCellIds from expressions inline rather than depending on reify-eval::deps
design_decisions.rationale: The collect_value_refs function in reify-eval/src/deps.rs does exactly what we need, but reify-constraints cannot depend on reify-eval (that would create a circular dependency). We'll implement a local version of the same walk in decompose.rs, or expose a shared utility via reify-types or reify-expr.

design_decisions.decision: No changes to reify-types for this task
design_decisions.rationale: ConstraintDomain and all needed types already exist in reify-types. The SolverRegistry and classifier are implementation concerns that belong in reify-constraints. The only structural change is adding the optional domain field to CompiledConstraint in reify-compiler.

reuse.how: Already defines Dimensional, Geometric, Logical, CrossDomain variants. Use directly for classification output.
SolverRegistry implements this trait, making it a drop-in replacement for DimensionalSolver in Engine.
Used as both the 'dimensional' and 'fallback' solver in SolverRegistry. No modifications needed.
Classifier walks this tree to determine leaf types and operator domains. Same traversal pattern as collect_value_refs in reify-eval/src/deps.rs.

The decomposer needs the same recursive walk to find which ValueCellIds a constraint references. Cannot import from reify-eval (circular dep), so will reimplement the same pattern locally using CompiledExprKind match arms.
SolverRegistry constructs sub-ResolutionProblems per component, filtering auto_params and constraints to the component's subset.

reuse.what: Test helper patterns (mm(), vcid(), cnid(), expression builders)
reuse.how: Reuse the same helper functions for constructing test expressions in new test files. Can also use CompiledExpr::literal(), ::value_ref(), ::binop() constructors.
No changes needed — Engine already accepts Box<dyn ConstraintSolver>. SolverRegistry can be boxed and passed directly.
Used by the classifier if needed to evaluate constant sub-expressions, and by tests to verify constraint satisfaction.
